name: Seafood Risotto
recipeIngredient: 1 red bell pepper, seeded and cut into one large piece
1/2 pound scallops or clams in the shell
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, heat the chicken stock to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer until ready to use.
2. In a large heavy pot, heat 4 tablespoons butter and 3 tablespoons olive oil over moderate heat. Small children can help by taking paper off butter, cutting 4 tablespoons with a butter knife, and dropping it into the pot. Supervise them closely around any heat source.
3. Add half of the onions to the pot and cook until clear, about 5 to 8 minutes. Older children can wipe off shiitake caps with a damp cloth, remove stems, cut ends, slice stems lengthwise, and then slice the caps.
4. Add the rice to the onions and stir to coat. Children can do this once they practice a controlled stirring motion.
5. Add 1/3 cup white wine and stir until completely absorbed. Constant stirring is required for a good risotto. Equip children with a long-handled ladle and deep oven mitt, then carefully dip the ladle into the broth and pour it into the rice, stirring continuously until the stock is fully absorbed. Repeat using as much stock as necessary until the rice is cooked through but not mushy.
6. Meanwhile, in a medium skillet, heat 2 tablespoons butter and 1 tablespoon olive oil until melted. Add the remaining onions and stir to coat. Children can cut the red bell pepper into strips and then crosswise into small even squares. Add these to the onion mixture and combine. Add the tomatoes and cook until any liquid evaporates. Add the shiitake mushrooms and cook until any liquid evaporates.
7. Add the fresh seafood (shrimp, scallops, or clams) and cook until just done. Stir the sautéed seafood and vegetables into the pot of risotto. Grated Parmesan can be stirred in or served on top at the table. Cover until ready for service.
Yield: 4 servings
